qa_pairs.question: What are the essential settings that need to be configured when launching a new Windows virtual machine (VM) in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: When launching a Windows VM, you need to define the boot source as an image, select the provided Windows image name, specify a device size of 30GB or more for the root drive, decide whether to delete the volume upon VM termination, select your SSH key pair, and ensure the default security group is checked.

qa_pairs.question: What initial configuration steps must a user perform using the console after a Windows virtual machine (VM) first boots up in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: After a Windows VM first boots, users must select the location, language, and keyboard settings and agree to the license terms using the console within the OpenStack dashboard before the VM will finish booting.

qa_pairs.question: In an OpenStack project, what is the distinction between a private IP address and a public IP address for a virtual machine (VM)?
qa_pairs.answer: A private IP address, typically in the form `192.168.X.Y`, enables VMs within the same OpenStack project to communicate with each other and connect to an internet gateway internally. A public IP address, however, allows outside services and tools to initiate contact with your VM, facilitating administrative tasks or serving web content from your personal computer.
